description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a dark gray to brown coloration and a rough, weathered surface. The rock features several prominent cracks and crevices running across its top and sides, giving it a segmented appearance. Scattered across its surface are small, pale spots that may be lichen or mineral deposits, adding to its natural, aged look. The rock's form is somewhat rounded but with angular facets and edges, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for a long time. There are no visible artificial modifications, but a small label or tag is present on one side, possibly for identification or cataloging. Its substantial size and mass make it suitable as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A large, rough, dark gray-brown rock with segmented cracks, pale spots, and a weathered, natural appearance.
description_view_0: The top and front of the rock are visible, showing deep cracks and several pale spots concentrated on the upper surface.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the elongated profile and the segmented, blocky structure, with a visible label or tag on the right.
description_view_2: The rock appears more angular from this angle, with the label more prominent and the cracks forming distinct sections.
description_view_3: The rear view shows a concentration of pale spots and a rough, uneven surface with deep crevices.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
